repo_prior_art.cite: No in-catalogue PartialID entry gives a Kendall-tau-band continuous-PoC sharp set; pid_benefit_concordance_band_v1 was a Spearman-band strict-benefit transport problem, not a single-copula-evaluation GPN interval.

published_prior_art.cite: ChaogeHanLiuWu2026 gives continuous observed-event GPN Fréchet/monotonicity bounds and copula sensitivity; NelsenQuesadaMolinaRodriguezLallenaUbedaFlores2001/NelsenUbedaFlores2004 give pointwise copula bounds, but not the causal population-counterfactual sharp-set theorem.

This is mostly the immediate containment of a Gaussian copula sensitivity class inside the all-copulas Kendall-band class; ChaogeHanLiuWu2026 and HeLi2026 are the closest parametric-copula comparators.
The Fréchet-Hoeffding no-dependence-information reduction is classical and is the benchmark recovered by ChaogeHanLiuWu2026 for continuous GPN.

repo_prior_art.cite: No repo PartialID/Stat entry gives EIFs for concordance-envelope attribution endpoints.

published_prior_art.cite: HeLi2026 derives EIFs for fixed parametric-copula ordinal functionals; DornGuoKallus2022 gives sharp-sensitivity orthogonal-estimation templates, not Kendall-envelope GPN endpoints.
Given the EIF, this is a standard cross-fitted one-step CLT pattern in the style of HeLi2026 and DML theory, adapted to the active-envelope endpoint.

repo_prior_art.cite: No repo entry gives uniform inference for a tau-indexed family-free copula-envelope curve.

published_prior_art.cite: ChernozhukovLeeRosen2013 supplies a general intersection-bound inference template and HeLi2026 gives pointwise fixed-copula bands; neither gives active-piece-switching Kendall-envelope uniform bands.

novelty_flags.label: related_work / gap for thm:sharp-tau-band-gpn
novelty_flags.one_line: UbedaFlores2025 is described as a modern survey/benchmark for Kendall attainability, but the verified arXiv record is a Gini's-gamma bounds note that only contrasts its result with known Kendall/Spearman/Blomqvist bounds.

accepted_scope_caveats.label: ass:conditional-tau-band
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: The Kendall-tau band is an elicited sensitivity restriction, not identified from the observed law.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: The sharp interval claim is accepted only conditional on the maintained conditional Kendall-tau band.

accepted_scope_caveats.label: thm:population-endpoint-eif / thm:regular-one-step-clt
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: The EIF and root-n CLT are conditional on active-piece regularity and tailored nuisance-rate assumptions.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: The inference rung is accepted as a regular-regime claim, not as a global nonsmooth-endpoint theorem.

accepted_scope_caveats.label: conj:uniform-tau-curve-inference
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: Uniform tau-curve inference at active-piece switches is explicitly open.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: The tau-uniform coverage claim is accepted only as the proposal's conjectural solve target.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Replace the UbedaFlores2025 survey framing with the primary Kendall-bound papers and use UbedaFlores2025 only as a narrow modern contrast about Gini's gamma quasi-copula behavior.
Add an explicit attainment/measurable-selection lemma for pasting pointwise Kendall extrema across X, so the sharp set is [theta_L, theta_U] rather than only a closed outer envelope.
State the uniform tau-curve conjecture with a separate finite-switch/anti-degeneracy and uniform nuisance-rate condition, instead of relying on scalar endpoint product-rate assumptions.
Add a boundary corollary comparing the population target to the observed-event GPN of ChaogeHanLiuWu2026 under constant propensity or explicit e(X)-reweighting.

recommended_next_step: Continue revise on this angle: fix the UbedaFlores2025 positioning and sharpen the selection/uniform-inference assumptions before Stage 0.
